data: Using Correct Tenses in Daily Conversation
Using Correct Tenses in Daily Conversation
Revise how to use correct tenses in daily conversation

message: Let's revise how to use past and present tenses correctly in daily conversation.
image_url:
button_text: Continue!

message: Remember the structure you learned:

"Yesterday I [past action] and today I am [present action]."

Simple past for yesterday, present continuous for today — this makes your speech clear and natural.
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/7216d41e-4b29-4f8b-ae9a-2811133877c1-58.-Using-Correct-Tenses-in-Daily-Conversation.jpg
button_text: Next!

message: Great! Now let's practice using this structure again.
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/7216d41e-4b29-4f8b-ae9a-2811133877c1-58.-Using-Correct-Tenses-in-Daily-Conversation.jpg
button_text: Start Practice!

Select the sentence that uses correct tenses

1.Yesterday I met with the client and today I am drafting the follow-up email.
2.Yesterday I am meeting the client and today I will draft the email for follow-up.

Correct answer: 1

Select the sentence that correctly describes a task sequence

1.Yesterday I reviewed the contractor invoices and today I am processing the payments.
2.Yesterday I review the contractor invoices and today I will process the payments.

Correct answer: 1
Yesterday&&I&&updated the&&sales&&database and&&today I&&am&&generating the&&weekly report.
Yesterday&&I&&met with&&the&&client and&&today I&&am&&drafting the&&follow-up email.
Yesterday&&I&&organised the&&team&&files and&&today I&&am&&creating the&&project folder.
Yesterday&&I&&fixed the&&software&&issue and&&today I&&am&&testing the&&updated version.
Yesterday&&I&&reviewed the&&contractor&&invoices and&&today I&&am&&processing the payments.

first_message: That was incredible! Now we're going to practice by answering few questions in English. Can we start?
prompt_id: 4d3f5213-fcc4-40f3-879d-051908377a31
questions: "'1. Your manager asks what you worked on yesterday and what you are doing now. How do you respond?'

'2. A colleague asks for a quick update on your progress. How do you describe it using past and present tenses?'

'3. Your friend asks what you did at work yesterday and what today looks like. How do you respond?'

'4. Your team leader asks for a brief daily status. How do you describe yesterday and today's tasks?'

'5. During a team meeting, your turn comes to share your update. How do you describe what you did yesterday and today?'"
